title: Forensic analysis of backend.ts and failed Amplify deployment events
description: Inspect git history for amplify/backend.ts and related stack identity changes; inspect AWS Amplify/CloudFormation deployment events for the failed staging run; produce root-cause summary and safe remediation options.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Starting investigation. Plan: 1) inspect git history around dashboard/amplify/backend.ts and stack/vault naming changes, 2) inspect Amplify deployment timeline and CloudFormation failure events for the specified failed staging deployment, 3) correlate with stack ownership/replacement behavior and provide root-cause + safe fix options.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Investigation findings:
- Git history shows no committed removal of the backup stack from dashboard/amplify/backend.ts. Search for DynamoDbBackupStack across history only shows introduction commit 44944131 and import-fix commit 8f214236.
- CloudFormation history shows the staging backup nested stack was present and then deleted: stack suffix JLKQE696FTT3 created on 2026-03-18 18:33 UTC and deleted on 2026-03-30 21:47 UTC.
- Deleted nested stack events show BackupVault logical resource DynamoDbBackupVaultDBAF7642 had DELETE_SKIPPED while backup plan/selection/role were deleted.
- Amplify CI job 93 (2026-03-31 14:05 PT) attempted CREATE_IN_PROGRESS for nested resource DynamoDbBackupStack4A7B7731, then failed creating DynamoDbBackupVaultDBAF7642 with AlreadyExists.
- CloudTrail shows an out-of-band CDK deployment at 2026-03-30 14:45 PT by principal aws-cdk-ryan (CDK toolkit user agent) against the same root stack, matching the time window where DynamoDbBackupStack was removed.
Conclusion: failure was caused by stack ownership drift from out-of-band deployment/template that removed DynamoDbBackupStack, not by a new committed backend.ts change in staging CI. The backup vault persisted and later collided on recreate.
